etymology.description: 言(言) (yán, "speech") + phonetic 肘(肘) = asking with words -> final meaning
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 討(tǎo) consists of the speech element 言(言) (yán, "speech") combined with the phonetic 肘(肘), reflecting the action of 'asking for' or 'demanding' through spoken words.
etymology.simplification: 討(討) was simplified to 讨(討) by reducing 訁(言) (yán, "speech") to 讠, maintaining the phonetic phonetic link.

examples.english: He went to ask for money.

examples.chinese: 我们不能总是讨别人的帮助(我們不能總是討別人的幫助)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bù néng zǒng shì tǎo biérén de bāngzhù.
examples.english: We cannot always ask for help from others.

examples.chinese: 她讨厌被人忽视(她討厭被人忽視)，所以总是努力表现自己(所以總是努力表現自己)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tǎoyàn bèi rén hūshì, suǒyǐ zǒng shì nǔlì biǎoxiàn zìjǐ.
examples.english: She hates being ignored, so she always tries to make herself known.
